title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, TTLs, and Invalidation · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 6094 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cache Key Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cache Key Identity, keep Cache Key Identity connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Compression Vary condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Compression Vary output and hide the violated cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Compression Vary.
